items.source: Salesforce field vs Avoma call 16 Sep 2025 naming SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER

items.quote: 0 buyer-side contact role(s) on this opp
I'm not driving anything... I'm not the project leader, the part of the CET governing the project priorities.
I'm the only stakeholder... not the part of the CET governing the project priorities.

metrics.narrative: Timothy Duprey confirmed an estimated $80-100M in tail spend on the 16 Sep 2025 call ('it's probably 80 to a 100,000,000. Absolutely.'), against which the rep modeled $4-8M of potential ANA savings using a 4.5-9% benchmark from an existing $190M-tail-spend customer. This is a real, buyer-confirmed number, but it has not been turned into an owned business case: no one at Chemours has committed to validating or acting on it.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Timothy Duprey is curious and engaged with the product story but stated plainly he is not the project driver and cannot see who the real decision-makers are ('I'm not driving anything... I'm not the project leader, the part of the CET governing the project priorities'). He has not been re-engaged since.

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is fully named, but it is an incumbent-displacement problem, not an active bake-off: SAP Ariba (P2P, underutilized beyond transactional invoicing since S/4HANA paused), Sirion (contract management, buyer-acknowledged as dashboard-poor), and JAGGAER (RFx/RFI/RFQ). No rival vendor is actively bidding against Zycus.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and specific: poor end-to-end visibility across direct and indirect spend and contracts, high off-contract ('Maverick') purchases despite SAP Ariba P2P, weak catalog governance, supplier risk/compliance gaps, and a fragmented stack of SAP Ariba (transactional-only since the S/4HANA migration paused), Sirion (contract management, inherited from a sales-org acquisition, described as lacking usable dashboards), and JAGGAER (RFx/RFI/RFQ). All pain points were voiced directly by Timothy Duprey on the 16 Sep 2025 call.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process exists at this stage; the deal has not reached commercials, let alone contracting mechanics.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified anywhere in Salesforce or the call record. Timothy Duprey explicitly disclaimed the role: 'I'd only could say that I'd be a stakeholder in it. I'm not driving anything.' He also confirmed governance sits with an internal CET group whose leadership he could not name.

decision_process.narrative: The process, to the extent one exists, runs through an internal CET (governance) group that prioritizes projects; Tim is a stakeholder in it but not on the committee that decides. No named leader, cadence, or approval path has surfaced.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria exist because no formal project has been chartered. The only signal is Tim's personal interest in AI/MS Teams integration and ERP-agnostic tooling, which is not the same as a documented buyer requirement.

cro_panel.intro: Chemours is an early-stage ($100K, Qualified, Pipeline) opportunity resting almost entirely on a single 16 Sep 2025 discovery/demo call with one self-described non-decision-maker, Timothy Duprey.; Real pain surfaced (an estimated $80-100M in tail spend against a modeled $4-8M in ANA savings) and SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER were named as the fragmented incumbent stack, but no economic buyer, champion, or project charter exists, and the buyer side has not engaged again in the ~296 days since.; The rep sent a renewed outreach on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days before this sweep); no buyer reply is yet on record

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: No champion; Timothy Duprey explicitly said on the 16 Sep 2025 call he is not driving the project and has no visibility into who does, and hasn't re-engaged since
No economic buyer surfaced; MEDDPICC economic_buyer = gap; project sits with an unnamed internal CET group, no sponsor identified
Displacement opportunity, not a live bake-off; competition confirmed but Chemours' SAP Ariba/JAGGAER/Sirion stack is incumbent/piecemeal, not an active competitive bid
Buyer timeline is speculative; no charter or budget confirmed; internal project status remains 'maybe 2026, we'll see' per vulnerabilities
Close date pushed 114 days; 16 Sep 2026 to 8 Jan 2027 move signals stall rather than advance

deal.next_step: Hope you are having a great week.I’m following up on our previous discussion regarding Chemours' procurement environment and your ongoing landscape transition. We previously touched on the complexities of managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER alongside your S/4 HANA journey, and how a unified suite could help streamline contract visibility and supplier risk.I wanted to check in and see how your project status and timeline are progressing, and if any new priorities have surfaced.As your team continues to navigate these systems, we would be glad to share a quick, tailored look at how our AI tools, like Merlin Intake, integrate smoothly with core ERP environments to simplify user adoption. If you or your team are open to a brief sync or a deep-dive on a specific module in the coming weeks, please let me know what your schedule looks like.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 16 Sep 2025, 294 days old, 185 days to close (Jan 2027). Time in Qualified stage unknown due to missing submission dates. Last activity 13 days ago (24 Jun 2026). The close date implies a mid-cycle commercial stage, yet the deal has no identified economic buyer, no decision process, no champion, and the sole contact explicitly stated he is not driving the project. A qualified deal 185 days from close with no buyer-side project structure is indefensible. Required pace to reach Vendor Selected by Q4 2026 (to support Jan 2027 close) demands forming a buying committee, mapping the CET governance group, and securing executive sponsorship within 60 days. Current trajectory shows no such velocity.

No champion: Timothy is an influencer at best, with no evidence of advocacy or access to budget authority
Buyer timeline is speculative: 'maybe we form a project in 2026, we'll see' (discovery call 12 Mar 2026)
No charter, no budget confirmed, no procurement mandate
Prior Ariba modernization stalled when S/4HANA migration paused; dependency unresolved
Last buyer call was 12 Mar 2026, 117 days ago; subsequent activity is rep-initiated outreach with no buyer response captured
Forecast category Pipeline with 185 days to close is appropriate only if this were genuinely qualified; current evidence shows it is pre-discovery
north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified stage with 185 days to close but no project sponsor, no budget, no charter, and a single non-driver contact. This is discovery disguised as a qualified pipeline deal.

flags: Single-threaded on a stated non-decision-maker for ~10 months; converting Timothy Duprey's interest into a real deal requires reaching the CET sponsor who actually governs the 2026 project he referenced.
No compelling event or funded charter exists yet; quantify the $4-8M savings opportunity Tim already validated so it becomes a business case the CET can act on.

No formal competitive review has started, giving Zycus a quiet window to pre-position against the fragmented Ariba/Jaggaer/Sirion stack before Chemours opens a wider evaluation that could include new vendors.
Multi-thread beyond Timothy Duprey: he is a self-described non-driver, so identify and engage the internal group's actual project leader before investing further in this single contact.
No economic buyer or executive sponsor is mapped; confirm who controls procurement-technology budget within the internal group to validate this is a real, fundable initiative.

Lead with the AI/Merlin efficiency story Timothy Duprey explicitly asked about, tied to his named pain points (tail-spend leakage, fragmented Ariba/Jaggaer/Sirion systems), to differentiate ahead of any formal evaluation.
No champion: Timothy has provided no introductions, no advocacy, and has not re-engaged in 117 days
No formal project, charter, or budget confirmed; buyer timeline is speculative ('maybe 2026')
No multi-person buying committee or CET governance group visibility
Staged as Qualified with $100k amount and Jan 2027 close, but evidence supports Discovery stage at best

Single-threading is the #1 blocker: Timothy Duprey is the only contact, is not powerful in the decision, and has gone unengaged for ~296 days — actively work to identify and reach the CET's actual leader.
No next milestone is scheduled: convert the 7 Jul 2026 check-in email into a booked call with a defined agenda (project status + request for a sponsor introduction) rather than leaving it open-ended.
best_practice_check.summary: The overriding lever on this deal is multi-threading: everything else (champion-building, business case, competitive positioning) is blocked until a second Chemours stakeholder is engaged.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus exists; the single call (16 Sep 2025) was an exploratory pitch, and the buyer's own framing was 'maybe 2026, we'll see' with no commitment.

competitive_position.summary: No active competitive bid exists. Chemours already runs SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER piecemeal; the opportunity is a displacement play against buyer-acknowledged gaps in each, not a live bake-off.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: a lot of it was gonna depend on us migrating to S4 HANA... now we're back and looking at... the management portal of using Ariba with mainly a transactional level
competitors.how_we_win: Position full-suite consolidation (sourcing, contracts, supplier risk) against Ariba's transactional-only usage, citing the PwC/Dow incumbent-displacement stories raised in the same call.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: we use YAGGER YAGGER for that or RFIs and RFRFQs and data gathering
competitors.how_we_win: Show unified sourcing/RFx inside one suite rather than a fourth disconnected tool, reducing the fragmentation Tim himself described.

competitors.name: Sirion
competitors.quote: it doesn't give you the dashboards or I mean, you get some, I'll say, some reports, but you don't get an effective dashboard
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Zycus's AI-driven contract risk dashboards and clause-level risk rating against Sirion's reporting-only experience, per the buyer's own dissatisfaction.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Chemours is an early-stage ($100K, Qualified, Pipeline) opportunity resting almost entirely on a single 16 Sep 2025 discovery/demo call with one self-described non-decision-maker, Timothy Duprey. Real pain surfaced (an estimated $80-100M in tail spend against a modeled $4-8M in ANA savings) and SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER were named as the fragmented incumbent stack, but no economic buyer, champion, or project charter exists, and the buyer side has not engaged again in the ~296 days since. The rep sent a renewed outreach on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days before this sweep); no buyer reply is yet on record.

deal_risk.text: No champion and no economic buyer at Qualified stage, with the deal stalled roughly ten months since its only substantive call (16 Sep 2025) — the classic early-stage risk pattern (weak/no champion, EB unmapped, single-thread, stalled engagement) is fully present.

win_position.text: No economic buyer or sponsor exists — the Qualified-stage deal has no named person with budget authority; on the 16 Sep 2025 call, sole contact Timothy Duprey confirmed 'I'm not driving anything... I'm not the project leader.'

Single-threaded for roughly 296 days — Timothy Duprey remains the only engaged contact since the 16 Sep 2025 discovery call; no second stakeholder has surfaced despite the rep's renewed outreach on 7 Jul 2026.

Real, quantified pain exists — Timothy Duprey named an estimated $80-100M in tail spend (16 Sep 2025), against which Zycus's ANA tool implies $4-8M of savings potential, a credible business case if a project is ever chartered.

packets.subject: Timothy Duprey

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: a lot of it was gonna depend on us migrating to S4 HANA... now we're back and looking at... the management portal of using Ariba with mainly a transactional level
value.how_we_win: Position full-suite consolidation (sourcing, contracts, supplier risk) against Ariba's transactional-only usage, citing the PwC/Dow incumbent-displacement stories raised in the same call.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: A prior Ariba modernization effort stalled when the S/4HANA migration paused
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the unified, AI-native platform that executes now while SAP's roadmap is uncertain; make Merlin Intake the Phase 1 quick-win tied to S/4HANA go-live, demonstrating faster time-to-value than restarting Ariba modernization.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: "Tail-spend leakage," poor catalog governance, and high off-contract purchases despite using Ariba P2P.
value.how_we_win: Position unified eProcurement + catalog governance to close the tail-spend and off-contract leakage Ariba has not solved.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: A lot of it was gonna depend on us migrating to S/4HANA... now we're back and looking at the management portal of using Ariba with mainly a transactional level.
value.how_we_win: Position the full source-to-pay suite (eProcurement/eInvoicing/Merlin Intake/ANA) as the modernization path that doesn't depend on the stalled S/4HANA migration.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba

value.name: JAGGAER
value.quote: we use YAGGER YAGGER for that or RFIs and RFRFQs and data gathering
value.how_we_win: Show unified sourcing/RFx inside one suite rather than a fourth disconnected tool, reducing the fragmentation Tim himself described.

value.name: JAGGAER
value.quote: Managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER as three disconnected platforms
value.how_we_win: Tail-spend plus core-sourcing in one platform with AI intake, vs JAGGAER as a separate tail-only tool.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: Lack of leveraging Ariba for RFX space (using Jaggaer instead) and challenges with supplier risk and performance management (KPIs, analytics).
value.how_we_win: Bring RFX/sourcing into the same platform as P2P to eliminate the separate Jaggaer workflow and its disconnected supplier-performance data.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: We use Jagger for that, or RFIs and RFQs and data gathering.
value.how_we_win: Show Zycus eSourcing/RFX automation consolidating Jaggaer's function onto the same platform as procurement and contracts.

packets.subject: Jaggaer

value.name: Sirion
value.quote: it doesn't give you the dashboards or I mean, you get some, I'll say, some reports, but you don't get an effective dashboard
value.how_we_win: Lead with Zycus's AI-driven contract risk dashboards and clause-level risk rating against Sirion's reporting-only experience, per the buyer's own dissatisfaction.

value.name: Sirion
value.quote: Managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER as three disconnected platforms
value.how_we_win: Consolidation story: replace Sirion CLM plus Ariba plus JAGGAER with one Zycus suite, eliminating integration tax and user friction.

value.name: Sirion
value.quote: Using a separate system (Sirion) for contract management, leading to separate databases and poor user experience/dashboards.
value.how_we_win: Consolidate contract lifecycle management into the same platform to remove the separate database and dashboard fragmentation Sirion causes.

packets.subject: Sirion
